label: Precision
description: The proportion of retrieved items that are relevant.
instrumentation: Collect human relevance judgments on retrieved items
Compute precision = relevant_retrieved / total_retrieved
Often reported at cutoff k (e.g., Precision@10)
validity_threat: Depends on the completeness and quality of relevance judgments; ignores unretrieved relevant items.

label: Recall
description: The proportion of all relevant items that are retrieved.
instrumentation: Enumerate the full set of relevant items (or a sample)
Count how many were retrieved by the system
Compute recall = relevant_retrieved / total_relevant
validity_threat: Hard to estimate when the full relevant set is unknown or incompletely judged.

label: F1 Score
description: Harmonic mean of precision and recall, balancing both in a single score.
instrumentation: Compute precision and recall first
Calculate harmonic mean of both
validity_threat: Assumes equal importance of precision and recall, which may not match stakeholder priorities.

label: Normalized Discounted Cumulative Gain (NDCG)
description: A ranking metric that rewards placing highly relevant items near the top, normalized by the ideal ranking.
instrumentation: Collect graded relevance judgments for retrieved items
Compute DCG using position-discounted gains
Normalize by the ideal DCG (IDCG)
validity_threat: Interpretation depends on gain and discount function choices; requires consistent graded judgments.

label: AUC-ROC
description: Area under the receiver operating characteristic curve, measuring the tradeoff between true positive and false positive rates across thresholds.
formula: AUC = area under ROC curve (TPR vs FPR)
instrumentation: Collect model predictions with confidence scores
Compare against ground truth labels
Compute TPR/FPR at multiple thresholds and integrate
validity_threat: Can be misleading with highly imbalanced classes.

label: Task Accuracy / Exact Match
description: Proportion of correct answers or exact matches against gold-standard labels across a benchmark.
instrumentation: Run model on benchmark dataset with gold labels
Compare model outputs to reference answers
Count exact matches or correct classifications
validity_threat: Depends on benchmark quality and coverage; may not reflect real-world performance.

label: Response Time / Latency
description: Time from user request to system response delivery.
instrumentation: Log request start timestamp
Log response end timestamp
Compute difference
validity_threat: Network latency may confound measurement; varies by query complexity.

label: Task Completion Time
description: Total time for a user to complete their analytic task end-to-end.
instrumentation: Log task start event
Log task completion event
Track across sessions if needed
validity_threat: Interruptions and multitasking can inflate times; task boundaries may be ambiguous.

label: Time to First Click
description: Time elapsed between presenting search results and the user's first click on a result.
instrumentation: Log timestamp when results are displayed
Log timestamp of first user click on a result
Compute difference
validity_threat: May reflect reading strategy, hesitation, or task difficulty rather than result quality alone.

label: Clicked Result Dwell Time
description: The amount of time a user spends viewing a clicked result before returning.
instrumentation: Log timestamp when user clicks a result
Log timestamp when user returns or ends session
Compute difference
validity_threat: Long dwell time can indicate engagement or confusion; short dwell can indicate success or dissatisfaction.

label: Source-Click Rate
description: How often users click through to view the original source cited by the AI.
instrumentation: Log clicks on citation/source links
Log total number of citations displayed
Divide clicks by citations shown
validity_threat: High click rate could mean users need to verify (low trust) or are genuinely interested.

label: Hallucination Rate
description: Proportion of AI-generated claims that are unsupported by source material or fabricated.
instrumentation: Sample AI outputs
Human or automated fact-checking against source documents
Count unsupported claims / total claims
validity_threat: Detection depends on source availability; automated checks may miss subtle hallucinations.

label: Trust Rating
description: User-reported trust in the system's outputs, typically on a Likert scale.
instrumentation: Add post-task Likert question: 'I trusted this answer'
Collect after each interaction or session
Aggregate across users and tasks
validity_threat: Susceptible to response bias, social desirability, and timing effects.

label: System Usability Scale (SUS)
description: Standardized 10-item questionnaire measuring perceived usability on a 0–100 scale.
instrumentation: Administer SUS questionnaire after user session
Score using standard SUS formula
Compare against known benchmarks (68 = average)
validity_threat: May not capture domain-specific usability issues; sensitive to timing of administration.

label: NASA Task Load Index (NASA-TLX)
description: Multidimensional self-report measure of perceived workload across six subscales (mental, physical, temporal demand, performance, effort, frustration).
instrumentation: Administer NASA-TLX questionnaire post-task
Compute composite or subscale scores
Compare across conditions or over time
validity_threat: Susceptible to response bias, timing effects, and interruption burden.

label: Coherence and Fluency Score
description: Automatic linguistic quality scoring of LLM-generated text for coherence, fluency, and readability.
instrumentation: Collect LLM-generated free text outputs
Score using automated metrics (BERTScore, ROUGE, BLEU, perplexity)
Optionally supplement with LLM-as-judge scoring
validity_threat: Automated scores may not correlate well with human quality judgments; proxy for true coherence.

label: Calibration Error (ECE)
description: Measures whether model confidence scores match actual correctness rates. A well-calibrated model that says it is 80% confident should be correct 80% of the time.
formula: ECE = weighted average of |confidence - accuracy| across bins
instrumentation: Collect model predictions with confidence/probability scores
Bin predictions by confidence level
Compare average confidence to actual accuracy in each bin
validity_threat: Requires access to model probabilities/logprobs; bin count and method affect results.

label: Robustness / Perturbation Degradation
description: Measures performance drop when inputs are perturbed (typos, dialect shifts, domain changes, noisy data).
instrumentation: Create perturbed versions of test inputs (typos, paraphrases, domain shifts)
Run model on both original and perturbed inputs
Measure performance drop or recovery
validity_threat: Robustness is inferred from degradation patterns; perturbation design affects conclusions.

label: Bias / Fairness Score
description: Assesses whether the system produces different quality outputs for different demographic groups, using bias benchmarks or counterfactual tests.
instrumentation: Run bias benchmarks with counterfactual/paired prompts
Slice results by demographic group when labels are available
Compute disparity metrics across groups
validity_threat: Difficult without demographic labels; bias benchmarks may not capture all real-world biases.

label: Toxicity / Safety Violation Rate
description: Quantifies the rate of harmful, toxic, or unsafe content generated by the model, using safety benchmarks and red-teaming prompts.
instrumentation: Run red-teaming prompt suites
Score outputs using toxicity classifiers or human reviewers
Report violation rates and severity distribution
validity_threat: Toxicity classifiers have their own biases; red-team prompts may not cover all attack vectors.

label: Jailbreak / Prompt Injection Resistance
description: Measures the model's resistance to adversarial prompts that attempt to bypass safety guardrails.
instrumentation: Run standardized jailbreak/prompt-injection attack suites
Classify each response as compliant or violated
Compute attack success rate and severity
validity_threat: Attack suites evolve rapidly; passing one suite doesn't guarantee robustness to novel attacks.

label: Efficiency / Cost-Quality Tradeoff
description: Characterizes the tradeoff between model quality and computational cost (latency, throughput, dollar cost, energy).
unit: cost per query / tokens per second
instrumentation: Measure latency and throughput per request
Track token counts and compute costs
Plot quality vs. cost tradeoff curves across models
validity_threat: Hardware differences make direct comparisons difficult; costs change as providers update pricing.

label: Helpfulness / Human Preference Rating
description: Measures perceived helpfulness via human ratings or pairwise preference comparisons between model outputs.
instrumentation: Collect human ratings on response quality (Likert or rubric)
Or run pairwise preference comparisons (A vs B)
Optionally use LLM-based graders as scalable proxy
validity_threat: Human preference is subjective and variable; LLM-based proxies may have systematic biases.

label: LLM-as-Judge Agreement
description: Meta-evaluation metric measuring how well an LLM judge's scores agree with human judgments.
instrumentation: Collect both LLM judge scores and human annotation scores on same outputs
Compute correlation (Pearson/Spearman) or agreement statistics
Analyze failure modes and systematic biases
validity_threat: High agreement on one dataset may not transfer; judges may share blind spots with the models they evaluate.

label: Agent Task Success Rate
description: Proportion of benchmark episodes where an autonomous agent successfully completes the assigned task end-to-end.
instrumentation: Run agent on benchmark task episodes
Check success/failure using benchmark-defined assertions
Report pass@1 or pass@k for reliability
validity_threat: Benchmark tasks may not represent real-world complexity; pass@k masks per-run reliability.

label: Tool-Use Correctness
description: Measures whether an agent selects the correct tool, provides valid arguments, and executes successfully.
instrumentation: Log all tool calls with arguments
Validate against schemas and ground truth sequences
Compute selection accuracy, argument correctness, and execution success
validity_threat: Correct tool selection doesn't guarantee correct task completion; schemas may not cover all valid approaches.

label: Planning and Reasoning Quality
description: Evaluates step-level correctness and overall plan quality in multi-step agent reasoning, using trace analysis or LLM-as-judge.
instrumentation: Collect agent action sequences and reasoning traces
Score using graph-based plan matching or LLM-as-judge
Evaluate intermediate outputs, not just final result
validity_threat: LLM judges are proxies for human judgment; multiple valid plans may exist for a single task.

label: Agent Observability / Trace Quality
description: Measures the quality and completeness of execution traces logged during agent task execution, supporting debugging and monitoring.
instrumentation: Log agent execution traces including tool calls and step outputs
Assess trace completeness and queryability
Score offline metric coverage from traces
validity_threat: Trace logging may miss internal reasoning steps not explicitly surfaced.

label: Multimodal Grounding / Visual Hallucination
description: Assesses whether generated text is properly grounded in visual evidence, detecting hallucinated descriptions not supported by the image.
instrumentation: Collect image-text pairs with generated descriptions
Score whether text claims are supported by visual content
Use grounding/hallucination benchmarks or human evaluation
validity_threat: Hallucination labels are often coarse; subtle grounding failures may be missed.

label: Multimodal Understanding Accuracy
description: Answer correctness on vision-language benchmarks measuring a model's ability to interpret image-text inputs.
instrumentation: Run model on vision-language benchmark datasets
Score using accuracy, VQA score, or SPICE
Compare against baseline models
validity_threat: Benchmark coverage may not reflect real-world multimodal tasks.

label: Explanation Faithfulness / Fidelity
description: Measures whether a post-hoc explanation (feature attributions, saliency maps) accurately reflects the model's actual decision-making process.
instrumentation: Perturb or remove features identified as important by the explanation
Check if model behavior changes as the explanation predicts
Score using AOPC, sufficiency, or comprehensiveness metrics
validity_threat: Faithfulness tests approximate true reasoning; no test can fully verify explanation correctness.

label: Explanation Usefulness / User Trust in Explanations
description: Measures how useful, trustworthy, and actionable users find AI explanations, using questionnaires and task performance.
instrumentation: Conduct user studies with explanation interfaces
Administer trust, perceived utility, and confidence questionnaires
Measure task performance and decision accuracy with and without explanations
validity_threat: Trust/workload scales are proxies; users may over-trust or under-trust irrespective of explanation quality.

label: Clinical Decision Accuracy with AI
description: Measures clinician decision accuracy when using AI-assisted clinical decision support, compared to unassisted decisions.
instrumentation: Conduct controlled clinical study (with/without AI)
Track clinical decision accuracy and time-to-diagnosis
Compare outcomes across conditions
validity_threat: Study conditions may not reflect real clinical workflow pressures; ethical constraints limit study design.

label: Implementation Readiness Score
description: Framework-based assessment of AI system readiness for real-world deployment, covering capability, utility, workflow fit, and adoption factors.
instrumentation: Apply implementation readiness framework (e.g., TEHAI checklist)
Assess evidence across validation, workflow fit, and ethical dimensions
Score each dimension and identify gaps
validity_threat: Framework scores approximate real-world impact; weightings may not match specific deployment contexts.

label: AI Trustworthiness Composite Score
description: Multi-dimensional framework score covering fairness, transparency, privacy, and reliability dimensions of AI trustworthiness.
instrumentation: Assess system behavior across fairness, transparency, and privacy dimensions
Score each dimension using appropriate sub-metrics
Compute composite trustworthiness score
validity_threat: Composite scores may mask critical weaknesses in individual dimensions.

label: RAG Faithfulness / Answer Relevance
description: Evaluates whether a RAG system's generated answers are grounded in retrieved documents and relevant to the question.
instrumentation: Collect question-context-answer triples
Score faithfulness: is the answer supported by retrieved context?
Score relevance: does the answer address the question?
Use frameworks like RAGAS for automated scoring
validity_threat: Automated faithfulness checks may miss subtle unsupported claims.

label: Functional Task Success (Test-Based)
description: Verifies agent task execution against deterministic test harnesses (e.g., unit tests, file diffs) in software engineering or structured tasks.
instrumentation: Run agent on real-world task instances (e.g., GitHub issues)
Verify output against unit tests or ground-truth file changes
Compute pass rate across test suite
validity_threat: Test suites may not cover all valid solutions; passing tests doesn't guarantee code quality.

label: Multi-Agent Communication Efficiency
description: Evaluates teamwork quality and communication efficiency in multi-agent systems using graph-theoretic analysis of interaction patterns.
instrumentation: Log interaction graphs and message logs between agents
Analyze communication topology using graph theory
Compute connectivity, information flow, and redundancy metrics
validity_threat: Graph metrics may not capture message quality or semantic content of agent communication.

label: Carbon Footprint / Energy Cost
description: Measures the environmental cost of model inference in terms of energy consumption and carbon emissions.
instrumentation: Log GPU wattage during inference
Track token counts and compute time
Apply carbon accounting formulas based on grid carbon intensity
validity_threat: Carbon intensity varies by location and time; measurement tools may be imprecise.

label: Evaluation Reproducibility / Protocol Quality
description: Assesses whether an evaluation can be reproduced by documenting prompts, decoding parameters, dataset provenance, and contamination checks.
instrumentation: Document all evaluation configuration (prompts, decoding, versions)
Run contamination/leakage checks on test data
Score protocol completeness against reproducibility checklist
validity_threat: Checklist completion does not guarantee actual reproducibility; undocumented factors may still vary.
